what_it_does: A design-system specimen page that displays the surface-zone registry: ten near-white semantic tones (base, content, panel, review, source, package, success, warning, reject, and the gold 'vi'/decision zone), each shown as a card with a wash swatch, a strong-variant chip, an ink dot, a role label, a name, and its surface token + mix percentage. Closes with a stated 'gold rule'. Links ../styles.css and builds the grid from a JS ZONES array.
observations: @dsCard group="Colors" header with name 'Surface-Zone Registry' and subtitle 'Low-opacity tonal zoning · gold reserved for decisions'.
Body background is a radial gradient of --zone-base-strong over --zone-base-surface.
ZONES array pairs each zone key with a role and a mix percentage: base 3.5%, content 3%, panel 5%, review 6%, source 5%, package 6.5%, success 6%, warning 7%, reject 6%, vi 9%.
Each card reads four tokens per zone (--zone-<k>-surface/-strong/-edge/-ink), with the 'vi' zone special-cased to --vi-surface/--vi-strong/--vi-edge and gold ink (--accent-gold).

Explanatory copy states near-white surfaces mark containment depth (cross-references core/containers.css) and that these ten semantic tones are an opt-in layer on top, scaled by one --zone-intensity knob.
A highlighted rule block states the 'gold rule': gold is the only saturated voice, reserved for Vi, active decisions, selected states, key emphasis, primary actions — never a default background.
Roles map zones to system concepts: Environment, Primary, Secondary, Decision, Provenance, Handoff, Ready, Incomplete, Anti-pattern, Vi/gold.
standouts: The explicit per-zone mix percentages (3%-9%) document the exact 'almost imperceptible' tonal ladder as data.
The codified 'gold rule' — gold reserved for decisions, never a default background — stated inline on the specimen.
Roles tie visual zones to product/workflow semantics (Provenance/Handoff/Decision/Anti-pattern), revealing a decision-workflow domain behind the colours.
conventions: Follows the specimen pattern: @dsCard header + self-contained HTML linking the shared stylesheet, grid built by inline JS.
All zone colours resolved via --zone-<k>-* tokens (and --vi-* for the gold zone); no raw zone hex in the page.
Uses the rotated-gold-gem motif in the header, consistent with sibling specimens.
Special-cases the 'vi' zone to the --vi-* token family rather than the --zone-* pattern (one deviation from the uniform naming).
concerns: The mix percentages are written as static strings in the JS array; if the actual token mixes in styles.css change, these displayed percentages could become stale (a second copy of the value).
notes: The specimen that documents the ten near-white semantic zone tones, their exact mix percentages and roles, and the canonical 'gold = decisions only' rule — the colour-meaning map of the system.
questions: Are the displayed mix percentages kept in sync with the real --zone-*-surface token definitions, or could they drift?
Why is the 'vi' zone modelled under --vi-* tokens instead of the uniform --zone-vi-* pattern used for the other nine?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the subtitle promises low-opacity tonal zoning with gold reserved for decisions, and the page renders the ten zone tones plus the explicit gold rule.
apparent_themes: Zoning as depth, not category (containment made visible)
Semantic tone as an opt-in layer over the depth ladder
Gold discipline (one saturated voice, reserved for decisions)
intention_signals: Trying to teach the rule that surfaces signal containment/role through barely-perceptible warm tints while gold stays scarce and meaningful — making the colour-meaning contract visible and checkable.
novelty: Exposing the exact low single-digit mix percentages per semantic zone, and tying zones to decision-workflow roles (Provenance/Handoff/Decision/Anti-pattern), is a distinctive way to document colour.
contribution: Contributes the canonical zone-tone registry with per-zone roles and mix percentages plus the codified gold-reservation rule — the system's colour-semantics map rendered as a verifiable specimen.
summary_for_embedding: A ConceptV design-system specimen HTML page documenting ten near-white semantic surface zones (with roles and exact mix percentages) and the gold-reserved-for-decisions rule, all resolved from --zone-* and --vi-* tokens.
intention_for_embedding: To make visible and checkable the system's colour-meaning contract: warm near-white zones encode containment depth/role while gold stays the single saturated decision voice.
